target.title: feat(fireworks): catalog DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B reasoning models
target.body:
**TL;DR:** I run OpenClaw against Fireworks serverless and wanted their current reasoning lineup — DeepSeek V4 Pro, MiniMax M2.7, GLM-5.1, GPT-OSS 120B — selectable like any bundled model instead of falling through to the dynamic-model guesswork. Cataloging DeepSeek V4 on Fireworks also trips a real bug: core's deepseek-native fallback injects `thinking` next to `reasoning_effort` and Fireworks rejects that request outright. The fix turned out to need zero new runtime code — one manifest compat field core already supports.

## Summary

**What does this add?** Four Fireworks serverless reasoning models to the bundled `extensions/fireworks` manifest catalog, each with `reasoning_effort` compat (supported efforts + level map) and a per-family thinking profile so thinking menus only offer levels the deployment actually accepts:

| model | thinking levels | default | context |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `deepseek-v4-pro` | off…max | high | 1,048,576 |
| `minimax-m2p7` | low/medium/high (no off) | medium | 196,608 |
| `glm-5p1` | binary off/on | on | 202,752 |
| `gpt-oss-120b` | low/medium/high | low | 131,072 |

**What does this fix?** Without the patch, selecting `fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro` with thinking enabled kills every turn. Core's DeepSeek V4 fallback (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts`) matches the id's last segment (`deepseek-v4-pro`) on any unowned OpenAI-compatible provider and injects `thinking: { type: "enabled" }` plus `reasoning_effort`. Fireworks rejects that payload:

```
{"error":{...,"message":"cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'"}}
```

**Why is one manifest field the right fix?** Core already built the seam for exactly this class of deployment — `compat.thinkingFormat` set to anything but `"deepseek"` suppresses the injected `thinking` entirely (even `thinking: {type:"disabled"}`) and strips deepseek replay fields, while `reasoning_effort` keeps flowing from the manifest map. The doc comment on `deepSeekV4NativeThinkingAllowedByCompat` names the use case ("deployments — notably Azure AI Foundry DeepSeek V4 — reject the `thinking` parameter outright"), and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` pins the whole behavior. So the deepseek-v4-pro row carries `"thinkingFormat": "openai"` and no stream wrapper is added; an earlier iteration of this branch carried a 75-line plugin `wrapStreamFn` + tests doing the same job, deleted in favor of the manifest seam per `extensions/AGENTS.md` ("before adding a new provider-local `wrapStreamFn` … check whether the same behavior already exists").

**Thinking `off` no longer escalates to max.** MiniMax M2.7 and GPT-OSS 120B are the first bundled profiles without an `off` level, and they exposed a core clamp quirk: when every supported level exceeds the request, `resolveSupportedThinkingLevelFromProfile` picked the *highest* level — so switching a thinking-off session onto these models would silently land on maximum reasoning spend. One-line core fix (`src/auto-reply/thinking.ts`): clamp down to the lowest non-off level instead, with a regression test.

**Dynamic ids too, with empirical red→green.** The catalog rows alone are not enough, for two verified reasons: (1) non-cataloged `deepseek-v4*` ids still hit core's id-family matcher (the 400 reproduced live through `openclaw agent --local` on the manifest-only iteration); (2) Fireworks' baseUrl classifies as a proxy-like endpoint (`resolveProviderEndpoint` → `custom`, probed against this branch), so the transport's detected defaults disable `reasoning_effort` — a dynamic id that shows a thinking menu would never encode the chosen level. `resolveFireworksDynamicModel` therefore merges each profile-matched family's manifest-row compat (single source of truth) into dynamic ids via `applyModelCompatPatch`, keeping menus and wire behavior 1:1 for all four families. Proven live at the e2e surface: `openclaw agent --local … --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high` failed with the exact 400 on the manifest-only iteration of this branch and returns the model reply with the dynamic patch in place.

**Where do the numbers come from?** Model ids and context windows verified against the live Fireworks API (`/v1/models`; `minimax-m2p7` is not in that account-scoped listing, so it was verified with a live 1-token completion plus the model page). Pricing verified against `https://docs.fireworks.ai/serverless/pricing` (incl. cache-read rates: deepseek 0.145, minimax 0.06, glm 0.26, gpt-oss 0.015).

## Linked context

- #90326 (open, same author) resolves catalog model limits from `openclaw.plugin.json` via core and refactors the same plugin files — complementary companion; this PR's live verification ran without it. Whichever lands second needs a textual rebase (both touch `model-id.ts`/`provider-catalog.ts`).
- Core seam + tests: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/extra-params.ts` (`deepSeekV4NativeThinkingAllowedByCompat`, non-native sanitizer), `extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts`.
- Sibling precedent: `extensions/deepseek/openclaw.plugin.json` ships the same `supportsReasoningEffort` manifest compat; pricing for `deepseek-v4-pro` matches the first-party row ($1.74/$3.48, cacheRead $0.145).
- Manifest compat keys (`thinkingFormat`, `supportedReasoningEfforts`, `reasoningEffortMap`) are existing config schema: `src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`, `src/config/types.models.ts`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Fireworks DeepSeek V4 Pro turns failing with 400 "cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'" when thinking is enabled; the three other reasoning families missing from the bundled catalog (no pricing, wrong default thinking levels via dynamic fallback).
Real environment tested: live Fireworks serverless API (`api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1`) with a real API key; OpenClaw checkout of this branch on Linux/Node 24.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `openclaw models list --all --provider fireworks --plain`, then one noninteractive agent turn per model, e.g. `openclaw agent --session-id fw-shot-ds -m "One sentence: why is the sky blue?" --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high` (and the minimax/glm/gpt-oss equivalents).
Evidence after fix: screenshot below; red→green at the same command: `openclaw agent --local --session-id fw-proof-ds -m "Say READY and nothing else." --model fireworks/accounts/fireworks/models/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high` → before: `error=LLM request failed … rawError=400 cannot specify both 'thinking' and 'reasoning_effort'`; after: `READY`. Raw API contract probe: a direct `POST /v1/chat/completions` for `deepseek-v4-pro` with `{"thinking":{"type":"enabled"},"reasoning_effort":"low"}` returns the same 400, while `{"reasoning_effort":"low"}` alone returns a normal completion — the patched plugin emits the latter shape.
Observed result after fix: all four models listed in the catalog with pricing; live one-turn agent runs on this branch return model replies for all four families (deepseek-v4-pro/minimax-m2p7/glm-5p1/gpt-oss-120b), including a deepseek-v4-pro turn at `--thinking max` through the dynamic-id path.
What was not tested: Fire Pass / `routers/*-fast` ids (separate follow-up), image input (all four rows are text-only per the live API), `xhigh`/`max` efforts end-to-end on deepseek (mapped per Fireworks' documented effort list but not exercised in a live turn).

<!-- SCREENSHOT HERE -->

## Tests and validation

- `extensions/fireworks` vitest: 17 passed (catalog compat incl. the new `thinkingFormat` assertion, per-family thinking profiles, dynamic deepseek-v4 compat merge, dynamic-model fallbacks); `src/auto-reply/thinking.test.ts`: 48 passed incl. the new no-off clamp regression; clamp consumers (`agent-command.live-model-switch`, cron model-override forwarding): green.
- `docs/providers/fireworks.md`: built-in catalog table and dynamic-id thinking notes refreshed with the new models.
- Core seam test: `extra-params.deepseek-v4-thinking-format.test.ts` 8 passed (pre-existing; proves the manifest override path this PR relies on).
- `tsgo` core + extension lanes clean; oxlint clean; oxfmt clean.
- Autoreview loop (claude-fable-5 thinking-high + codex gpt-5.5 medium), eight+ rounds; every actionable finding was folded in: docs refresh, dynamic-id family compat (codex caught that the proxy-like endpoint disables detected `reasoning_effort` — confirmed by probing `resolveProviderEndpoint`/`detectOpenAICompletionsCompat` on this branch), compat merge via `applyModelCompatPatch` (template compat preserved), core off-clamp fix, matcher scoping to verified versions (m2p7+/GLM-5+), `minimal` dropped from the deepseek menu. Final verdicts: claude "patch is correct (0.85)" across every round; codex "patch is correct (0.83), no actionable findings". One claude P3 deliberately accepted as forward-hypothetical: dynamic v4 ids inherit v4-pro's xhigh/max enum, which only matters if Fireworks ever ships another serverless v4 variant with a narrower effort enum (none exists today; the live `/v1/models` listing shows v4-pro only).

## Risk checklist

- Additive manifest rows + one compat field; no core or plugin runtime code changes (`model-id.ts`/`thinking-policy.ts` additions are plugin-local and only extend the existing kimi pattern).
- No config/default surface changes outside the plugin manifest; no migrations needed; existing configured models are unaffected (catalog rows are additive, ids unchanged).
- Dynamic (non-cataloged) Fireworks ids keep the exact pre-PR behavior except `deepseek-v4*`, which gains the compat opt-out (those turns 400'd before, so nothing working changes); `glm-5p1` moves from dynamic guesswork to a manifest row, which only adds pricing/compat it previously lacked.
- Thinking-profile levels are clamped to what Fireworks accepts per family, so a user-selected unsupported level can no longer produce a rejected request. The GLM profile applies to GLM-5+ ids only (the verified family); dynamic GLM-4.x ids keep the generic menu.
- Core clamp change is strictly cost-reducing: below-range requests now resolve to the lowest supported level instead of the highest; no existing test pinned the old escalate-up behavior.
